steps.title: Use the perpendicularity property
steps.content:
A tangent line to a circle is perpendicular to the radius at the point of tangency. The radius goes from the center $(-2, 2)$ to the tangent point $(3, -1)$.

Radius slope: $$m_{\text{radius}} = \frac{-1-2}{3-(-2)} = \frac{-3}{5}$$

Since the tangent line is perpendicular, its slope is the negative reciprocal: $$m_{\text{tangent}} = \frac{5}{3}$$

steps.title: Backsolve to find which point lies on the tangent line
steps.content:
The tangent line passes through $(3, -1)$ with slope $\frac{5}{3}$. A point lies on this line if the slope from $(3, -1)$ to that point equals $\frac{5}{3}$.

Backsolving — testing choice C $(6, 4)$:
$$\text{Slope} = \frac{4-(-1)}{6-3} = \frac{5}{3}$$

This matches the tangent line's slope. That matches choice C — C is correct.
